steps.title: Calculate base area and side length
steps.content: Total surface area equals base area plus lateral surface area. The base area is $42,120 - 25,220 = 16,900$ square inches. Since the pyramid has a square base, $s^2 = 16,900$, which gives $s = 130$ inches.

steps.title: Find the slant height from lateral surface area
steps.content:
The four lateral faces are triangles with base $s$ and height $\ell$ (the slant height). Their combined area is $2s\ell = 25,220$. With $s = 130$:
$$260\ell = 25,220$$
$$\ell = 97 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Use Pythagorean theorem to find pyramid height
steps.content:
In a right square pyramid, the slant height $\ell$, pyramid height $h$, and the distance from the base center to an edge midpoint ($s/2 = 65$) form a right triangle. By the Pythagorean theorem:
$$h^2 + 65^2 = 97^2$$
$$h^2 + 4,225 = 9,409$$
$$h^2 = 5,184$$
$$h = 72 \text{ inches}$$
That confirms A is correct.
